tool_args.label: Bridges and Tunnels Key Performance Metrics - October 2025
tool_args.raw_text: BRIDGES AND TUNNELS KEY PERFORMANCE METRICS - October 2025

payload.title: Bridges and Tunnels Key Performance Metrics
payload.description: Performance metrics document prepared for the October 2025 meeting of the Bridges and Tunnels Committee.

evidence_refs.source_quote: BRIDGES AND TUNNELS KEY PERFORMANCE METRICS
October 2025
This performance metrics document was prepared for the October 2025 meeting of the Bridges and Tunnels Committee.

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

tool_args.label: MTA Bridges and Tunnels
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Bridges and Tunnels is comprised of over 1,000 employees and operates seven bridges and two tunnels in New York City, handling over 330 million vehicle crossings every year in addition to managing the Congestion Relief Zone.

payload.description: Operates seven bridges and two tunnels in New York City, handling over 330 million vehicle crossings every year in addition to managing the Congestion Relief Zone.

evidence_refs.source_quote: MTA Bridges and Tunnels is comprised of over 1,000 employees and operates seven bridges and two tunnels in New York City, handling over 330 million vehicle crossings every year in addition to managing the Congestion Relief Zone.

tool_args.label: Catherine Sheridan, President of Bridges and Tunnels
tool_args.raw_text: Catherine Sheridan President, Bridges and Tunnels

evidence_refs.source_quote: Catherine Sheridan President, Bridges and Tunnels

tool_args.label: MTA Police Department
tool_args.raw_text: The MTAPD has supplemented patrol duties at the Henry Hudson, Marine Parkway–Gil Hodges Memorial, and Cross Bay Veterans Memorial bridges, enhancing safety and security for both our employees and the traveling public.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The MTAPD has supplemented patrol duties at the Henry Hudson, Marine Parkway–Gil Hodges Memorial, and Cross Bay Veterans Memorial bridges

tool_args.label: MTA Construction & Development
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Construction & Development's B&T Business Unit continues to make strong progress on a $154 million design-build contract for upgrades to the existing tower fender system at the Throgs Neck Bridge.

payload.description: MTA Construction & Development's B&T Business Unit works on Bridges and Tunnels capital projects.

evidence_refs.source_quote: MTA Construction & Development's B&T Business Unit continues to make strong progress on a $154 million design-build contract

tool_args.label: Rockland County Sheriff's Office
tool_args.raw_text: with the recent inclusion of the Rockland County Sheriff's Office, the multi-agency task force is now comprised of 17 partner agencies.

evidence_refs.source_quote: with the recent inclusion of the Rockland County Sheriff's Office, the multi-agency task force is now comprised of 17 partner agencies.

tool_args.label: Metropolitan Transportation Authority
tool_args.raw_text: The Metropolitan Transportation Authority is North America's largest transportation network, serving a population of 15.3 million people across a 5,000 square-mile travel area surrounding New York City through Long Island, southeastern New York State, and Connecticut.

payload.description: North America's largest transportation network, serving a population of 15.3 million people across a 5,000 square-mile travel area.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The Metropolitan Transportation Authority is North America's largest transportation network, serving a population of 15.3 million people across a 5,000 square-mile travel area

tool_args.label: Throgs Neck Bridge Fender Protection
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Construction & Development's B&T Business Unit continues to make strong progress on a $154 million design-build contract for upgrades to the existing tower fender system at the Throgs Neck Bridge. The contract is now 65% complete and is on track for early completion and within budget.

payload.description: Upgrades to the existing tower fender system at the Throgs Neck Bridge. $154 million contract. Includes painting of tower interiors/exteriors, new FDNY fireboat moorings, tower elevator rehabilitation, tower lighting replacement, and main cable inspection.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Throgs Neck Bridge Fender Protection On Track for Early Completion
MTA Construction & Development's B&T Business Unit continues to make strong progress on a $154 million design-build contract
New FDNY fireboat moorings and dry fire standpipe systems... Rehabilitation of the tower elevators; Replacement of tower interior lighting... and Inspection of the bridge's main cables.

issues: evidence_refs[2].source_quote is not present in the cited block

tool_args.label: Throgs Neck Bridge Fender Protection
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Construction & Development's B&T Business Unit continues to make strong progress on a $154 million design-build contract for upgrades to the existing tower fender system at the Throgs Neck Bridge. The contract is now 65% complete and is on track for early completion and within budget.

payload.description: Upgrades to the existing tower fender system at the Throgs Neck Bridge. $154 million contract. Includes painting of tower interiors/exteriors, new FDNY fireboat moorings, tower elevator rehabilitation, tower lighting replacement, and main cable inspection.

evidence_refs.source_quote: MTA Construction & Development's B&T Business Unit continues to make strong progress on a $154 million design-build contract for upgrades to the existing tower fender system at the Throgs Neck Bridge
the existing fender systems at the bridge's Bronx and Queens towers are being removed and replaced by a new precast concrete fender system
the following related work scope items were bundled together with the fender system project and painting work under this contract

tool_args.label: Total traffic decrease September 2025 over August
tool_args.raw_text: Total traffic at MTA Bridges and Tunnels facilities decreased by 1,494,842 vehicles or 4.9% in September 2025 over August traffic

payload.scope: MTA Bridges and Tunnels all facilities

evidence_refs.source_quote: Total traffic at MTA Bridges and Tunnels facilities decreased by 1,494,842 vehicles or 4.9% in September 2025 over August traffic

tool_args.label: Total collision rate September 2025
tool_args.raw_text: In September 2025, MTA Bridges and Tunnels achieved a total collision rate of 2.4 per million vehicles, a 20% reduction from the 3.0 per million vehicles recorded in September of 2024.

payload.raw_value_text: total collision rate of 2.4 per million vehicles

evidence_refs.source_quote: In September 2025, MTA Bridges and Tunnels achieved a total collision rate of 2.4 per million vehicles, a 20% reduction from the 3.0 per million vehicles recorded in September of 2024.

tool_args.label: Collisions with injury rate September 2025
tool_args.raw_text: the collisions with injury rate also declined, with a reported rate of 0.7 per million vehicles, reflecting a 12.5% decrease from the same period last year (0.8 per million vehicles).

evidence_refs.source_quote: the collisions with injury rate also declined, with a reported rate of 0.7 per million vehicles, reflecting a 12.5 % decrease from the same period last year (0.8 per million vehicles).

tool_args.label: Lost-time injury rate August 2025
tool_args.raw_text: The lost-time injury rate for August 2025 was 4.73, which constitutes a 12% increase relative to August 2024, when the rate was 4.22.

payload.raw_value_text: lost-time injury rate for August 2025 was 4.73

evidence_refs.source_quote: The lost-time injury rate for August 2025 was 4.73, which constitutes a 12 % increase relative to August 2024, when the rate was 4.22.

tool_args.label: Average incident response time September 2025
tool_args.raw_text: the average incident response time in September 2025 was reported at 6.0 minutes, marking a 16.6% improvement compared to 7.2 minutes in September 2024.

evidence_refs.source_quote: the average incident response time in September 2025 was reported at 6.0 minutes, marking a 16.6 % improvement compared to 7.2 minutes in September 2024.
